{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |King's Gambit Accepted| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd| |= | | | | | | | |pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | |pl|pd| | |= | | | | | |nl| | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl| |rl|= |parent=[[Chess/King's Gambit|King's Gambit]] |moves=1.e4 e5 2.f4 exf4 3.Nf3 h6 |eco=[[Chess/ECOC|C34]] }} =Becker's Defence= The idea of Becker's Defence 3...h6 is to play 4...g5 without having to fear 5.h4, which would simply be answered with 5...Bg7. We do not need to worry about 4.d4 as 4...g5 can secure the f4-g5-h6 pawn chain. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. 1.e4 e5 2.f4 exf4 3. Nf3 h6
